metadata.contractTitle: Statement of Work - Physician Diagnostic Support Tool using AI Rules Engine
metadata.pricingFull: Fixed Price with milestone-based invoicing totaling USD 425,000 across five milestones; AWS Assess and Mobilize funding mechanics apply separately per AWS program eligibility
metadata.governingAgreement: Master Services Agreement dated 01-Jun-2026 between INVKLM and CTIND
metadata.emLevel: EM4 – Fixed Price: The contract is explicitly labeled 'Fixed Price with milestone-based invoicing' with a total fixed price of USD 425,000. CTIND owns delivery of defined scope, milestones, deliverables, and acceptance criteria. The supplier bears budget and scope ownership risk within the fixed price. Milestone payments are triggered by acceptance or deemed acceptance, not by time/materials consumed. Change control is required for scope expansion. These obligations clearly establish EM4 – Fixed Price engagement model.
metadata.billingCadence: Milestone-based invoicing across five milestones; undisputed invoices payable within thirty (30) days from receipt of invoice per the Agreement or as stated
metadata.terminationTerms: CTIND entitled to payment for all Services performed up to effective date of expiry or termination including fees for accepted milestones, work in progress, and any pre-approved investments and third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of INVKLM
metadata.securityTerms: Role-based access control, secure data handling, encryption approach, audit logging, and secure configuration handling aligned to Customer-provided security requirements; Customer responsible for determining applicable legal, privacy, clinical, medical device, and regulatory obligations
metadata.slaTerms: Rule execution and output generation for a standard patient record should target response within 5 seconds in the agreed test environment, subject to dataset size and infrastructure performance; Production availability SLAs are excluded unless separately agreed
metadata.scopeChangeTerms: Changes in scope of Services, deliverables, acceptance criteria, assumptions, dependencies, commercial terms, or timelines only through written change order or amendment signed by authorized representatives of both parties; additional parameters, diagnostic categories, integrations, advanced AI/ML model development, production support, security certification, or regulatory support not expressly included shall be treated as change request
metadata.chargeableEvents: Additional fees chargeable through change management process if delivery of any milestone is delayed due to causes attributable to INVKLM; scope expansion, clinical rule changes after approval, AWS funding dependency changes, infrastructure/security changes outside approved design require impact assessment and change order
metadata.exitObligations: Deployment readiness package includes release notes, runbook, configuration guide, known issues, KT material, and handover checklist; UAT support, final handover, and closure report included in Milestone 5
metadata.executionEvidence: Signature block present for both parties with fields for Authorized Signatory, Name, Title, and Date; actual signatures not visible in uploaded contract

rows.section: Section 13.1 Fixed Price Fees Payable by INVKLM
rows.deliverable: All Services and deliverables under SOW
rows.measurement: Milestone-based invoicing across five milestones
rows.clause: In consideration of the Services under this fixed price SOW, INVKLM shall pay CTIND total fees of USD 425,000
rows.customerDependency: Timely milestone acceptance or deemed acceptance to trigger invoicing; Customer sign-offs for discovery, design, clinical rules, UAT, and milestones
rows.exclusions: AWS Assess and Mobilize funding are separate from Customer payment obligations unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing
rows.recommendedAction: Validate that AWS funding eligibility does not waive Customer payment obligations; confirm payment terms from MSA; establish milestone acceptance governance
rows.evidence: In consideration of the Services under this fixed price SOW, INVKLM shall pay CTIND total fees of USD 425,000
rows.reason: Fixed price of USD 425,000 is the primary commercial commitment; clarity needed on interaction between Customer invoices and AWS funding to avoid payment disputes

rows.target: 10 business days
rows.measurement: From delivery of each milestone; written acceptance or non-conformance required; deemed accepted if no response within period
rows.clause: Within ten (10) business days from delivery of each milestone, INVKLM shall complete user acceptance testing for the relevant milestone as per the acceptance criteria mutually agreed between the parties and provide written acceptance or non-conformance for the same. If INVKLM fails to provide written acceptance or non-conformance within the Acceptance Period, the milestone shall be deemed accepted by INVKLM.
rows.valueAtRisk: Up to USD 425,000 (all milestone payments)
rows.customerDependency: Customer must complete UAT and provide written acceptance or non-conformance within 10 business days; failure triggers deemed acceptance and invoice
rows.exclusions: Changes to approved scope, rules, requirements, acceptance criteria, or dependencies managed through change control, not acceptance process
rows.recommendedAction: Establish clear UAT governance, acceptance criteria sign-off, and escalation process; ensure Customer stakeholders understand deemed acceptance risk; document acceptance criteria for each milestone before delivery
rows.evidence: If INVKLM fails to provide written acceptance or non-conformance within the Acceptance Period, the milestone shall be deemed accepted by INVKLM.
rows.reason: Deemed acceptance after 10 business days creates payment obligation even if Customer has not validated deliverables; short window may be insufficient for complex clinical validation

rows.deliverable: AWS funding based on ARR milestones
rows.target: USD 5,000,000 ARR established by AWS; Milestone 1 at USD 10,000 ARR (CTIND requests USD 400,000); Milestone 2 at 20% of ARR (CTIND requests USD 100,000)
rows.measurement: Consumption in applicable AWS account ID calculated as Annual Recurring Revenue through joint exercise by CTIND, INVKLM, and AWS
rows.clause: The ARR for this project is established by AWS at USD 5,000,000 for the purpose of the Mobilize funding reference under this SOW. CTIND will procure funding from AWS based on work performed and AWS eligibility, per the following milestone schedule applicable to both SOWs: Milestone 1 Customer reaches USD 10,000 ARR. CTIND eligible to request first payment from AWS = USD 400,000. Milestone 2 Customer reaches 20% of ARR. CTIND eligible to request remaining funding from AWS = USD 100,000.
rows.valueAtRisk: USD 500,000 (AWS funding exposure)
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide account ID details, AWS consumption information, sign-offs, and documents reasonably required for AWS funding submissions; Customer must reach USD 10,000 ARR and 20% of ARR thresholds
rows.exclusions: AWS funding subject to AWS program requirements, AWS approval, required documentation, Customer sign-off, account consumption validation, ARR calculation, and AWS process timelines; CTIND not responsible for AWS approval decisions outside its control
rows.owner: INVKLM AWS Contact / CTIND AWS Program Lead
rows.recommendedAction: Clarify whether AWS funding failure affects Customer payment obligations; establish joint AWS consumption tracking and ARR validation process; document Customer cooperation obligations for AWS submissions; confirm ARR calculation methodology and account ID mapping before project start
rows.evidence: The ARR for this project is established by AWS at USD 5,000,000 for the purpose of the Mobilize funding reference under this SOW.
rows.reason: USD 5,000,000 ARR target is extremely high relative to USD 425,000 SOW value; achieving 20% of ARR (USD 1,000,000) may be unrealistic within 7-month project timeline; AWS funding eligibility depends on Customer consumption and AWS approval outside CTIND control; contract states AWS funding does not waive Customer payment but ambiguity remains

rows.section: Section 13.4 AWS Funding Dependencies and Clarifications
rows.target: Customer invoices remain payable as per Agreement and SOW regardless of AWS funding outcome
rows.measurement: Unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing, AWS funding eligibility does not waive Customer payment obligations
rows.clause: Unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing, AWS funding eligibility does not waive Customer payment obligations under this SOW. Customer invoices remain payable as per the Agreement and this SOW.
rows.customerDependency: Customer must pay invoices per milestone acceptance regardless of AWS funding outcome
rows.exclusions: AWS funding is separate from Customer payment unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing
rows.recommendedAction: Obtain written confirmation from Customer that payment obligations are independent of AWS funding; clarify what 'expressly agreed otherwise in writing' means and whether any side agreement exists; establish escalation process if AWS funding fails
rows.evidence: Unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing, AWS funding eligibility does not waive Customer payment obligations under this SOW.
rows.reason: The phrase 'unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing' creates ambiguity about whether a side agreement exists that makes Customer payment contingent on AWS funding; if AWS funding fails, Customer may dispute payment obligations

rows.deliverable: Blood test parameters, reference ranges, clinical rules, sample datasets
rows.target: Timely provision by Customer
rows.measurement: INVKLM shall provide approved blood test parameter list, reference ranges, sample anonymized datasets, clinical rules, expected diagnostic categories, and physician review feedback required for implementation and testing
rows.clause: INVKLM shall provide approved blood test parameter list, reference ranges, sample anonymized datasets, clinical rules, expected diagnostic categories, and physician review feedback required for implementation and testing.
rows.valueAtRisk: USD 425,000 (project delivery at risk)
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide clinical content, data, and SME approvals; delays attributable to Customer entitle CTIND to revise delivery schedule and charge additional fees through change management
rows.exclusions: CTIND does not independently validate medical efficacy beyond agreed software testing and support activities
rows.remedy: CTIND entitled to revise delivery schedule and charge additional fees through change management process if Customer delays
rows.recommendedAction: Establish detailed Customer deliverable schedule with dates and owners; define what constitutes 'timely' provision; document escalation process for Customer delays; clarify additional fee calculation methodology for Customer-caused delays
rows.evidence: INVKLM shall provide approved blood test parameter list, reference ranges, sample anonymized datasets, clinical rules, expected diagnostic categories, and physician review feedback required for implementation and testing.
rows.reason: Project success depends entirely on Customer providing clinical content and data; no specific timeline or SLA for Customer deliverables; Customer delays can trigger additional fees but calculation method unclear

rows.target: CTIND assumes no clinical responsibility; INVKLM responsible for medical review, validation, clinical governance, regulatory assessment, production release approval, and intended-use determination
rows.measurement: Final diagnosis, treatment determination, clinical interpretation, and patient care decisions remain exclusively with licensed healthcare professionals designated by INVKLM
rows.clause: CTIND shall not assume clinical responsibility for diagnoses, treatments, prescriptions, patient outcomes, or physician decisions made using outputs generated by the system. INVKLM shall be responsible for medical review, validation, clinical governance, regulatory assessment, production release approval, and intended-use determination.
rows.valueAtRisk: Unlimited (potential patient harm liability)
rows.customerDependency: Customer must ensure licensed healthcare professionals review all outputs; Customer responsible for regulatory compliance, medical device classification, clinical validation, and patient safety
rows.exclusions: CTIND excluded from clinical responsibility, patient outcomes, autonomous diagnosis, treatment prescription, medication recommendation, triage decisions, emergency alerts, medical device registration, clinical safety case
rows.recommendedAction: Validate that Customer has appropriate clinical governance, regulatory, and legal review processes; confirm Customer understands it owns all clinical responsibility and regulatory compliance; ensure production deployment includes physician review workflow and clinical disclaimers; obtain Customer acknowledgment of clinical responsibility allocation in writing
rows.evidence: CTIND shall not assume clinical responsibility for diagnoses, treatments, prescriptions, patient outcomes, or physician decisions made using outputs generated by the system.
rows.reason: Solution generates diagnostic suggestions that could influence patient care; if system outputs are used without proper physician review or if regulatory classification is incorrect, patient harm could occur; CTIND liability exclusion may not be enforceable if system defects contribute to patient harm; Customer may lack clinical governance maturity to manage AI-based clinical decision support

rows.deliverable: Medical device registration, regulatory submission, clinical validation
rows.target: Not included in SOW
rows.measurement: Independent third-party penetration testing, HIPAA/GDPR legal advisory, SaMD regulatory submission, ISO certification, SOC audit, or production managed services beyond agreed handover excluded unless separately contracted
rows.clause: Independent third-party penetration testing, HIPAA/GDPR legal advisory, SaMD regulatory submission, ISO certification, SOC audit, or production managed services beyond agreed handover.
rows.valueAtRisk: Regulatory non-compliance risk; potential additional costs for compliance activities
rows.customerDependency: Customer must determine applicable regulatory requirements and procure necessary compliance activities separately
rows.exclusions: Final medical decision-making, clinical validation, regulatory classification determination, medical device registration, clinical safety case, and patient outcome responsibility excluded from CTIND scope
rows.recommendedAction: Confirm Customer has engaged regulatory, legal, and compliance advisors; validate Customer understands solution may require medical device registration (FDA, EU MDR) depending on intended use; ensure Customer has plan for HIPAA, GDPR, penetration testing, and security certifications before production deployment
rows.evidence: Independent third-party penetration testing, HIPAA/GDPR legal advisory, SaMD regulatory submission, ISO certification, SOC audit, or production managed services beyond agreed handover.
rows.reason: AI-based diagnostic support tool may be classified as Software as a Medical Device (SaMD) requiring FDA or other regulatory approval; HIPAA compliance mandatory for PHI handling; exclusion of these activities creates regulatory and legal risk for Customer; Customer may not realize compliance obligations until late in project

rows.section: Section 16 Security, Privacy, and AI Governance
rows.target: Customer shall provide anonymized, pseudonymized, or authorized data for development and testing; Production PHI/PII access subject to Customer-approved controls
rows.measurement: Customer responsible for ensuring test data is anonymized, pseudonymized, or otherwise authorized for use
rows.clause: INVKLM shall remain responsible for determining applicable legal, privacy, clinical, medical device, and regulatory obligations for the intended use of the solution. Customer shall provide anonymized, pseudonymized, or authorized data for development and testing. Production PHI/PII access is subject to Customer-approved controls.
rows.valueAtRisk: HIPAA violation penalties (up to USD 1.9 million per violation category per year); reputational damage
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide properly anonymized or authorized data; Customer must approve production PHI/PII access controls
rows.exclusions: CTIND implements controls aligned to Customer-provided requirements but Customer determines applicable legal and privacy obligations
rows.owner: INVKLM Privacy Officer / Data Protection / Legal
rows.recommendedAction: Establish data handling protocol with Customer before project start; validate Customer has legal authority to provide data for development/testing; confirm data anonymization/pseudonymization methodology meets HIPAA Safe Harbor or Expert Determination standards; document Customer approval of production PHI/PII access controls; ensure Business Associate Agreement (BAA) is in place if PHI will be accessed
rows.evidence: Customer shall provide anonymized, pseudonymized, or authorized data for development and testing. Production PHI/PII access is subject to Customer-approved controls.
rows.reason: Healthcare data handling creates HIPAA compliance obligations; if Customer provides inadequately anonymized data or if production system accesses PHI without proper controls, both parties face regulatory penalties; responsibility allocation is clear but execution risk remains high

rows.target: Milestone deliverables shall materially conform to agreed requirements, acceptance criteria, clinical rules catalog, design documents, and milestone scope; No mutually agreed Severity 1 or Severity 2 defect should remain open unless approved workaround or deferral documented
rows.measurement: Material conformance to agreed specifications; Severity 1 and 2 defects resolved or deferred with approval
rows.clause: Milestone deliverables shall materially conform to the agreed requirements, acceptance criteria, clinical rules catalog, design documents, and milestone scope approved for that milestone. No mutually agreed Severity 1 or Severity 2 defect should remain open for the relevant milestone unless an approved workaround or deferral is documented.
rows.valueAtRisk: Quality and usability of deliverables; potential rework costs
rows.customerDependency: Customer must define and approve acceptance criteria, requirements, clinical rules, and design documents before milestone delivery; Customer must participate in defect severity classification
rows.exclusions: Changes to approved scope, rules, requirements, acceptance criteria, or dependencies managed through change control
rows.remedy: CTIND shall remediate defects within agreed milestone scope; changes to approved scope require change control
rows.owner: INVKLM UAT Lead / CTIND Quality Lead
rows.recommendedAction: Define defect severity classification (Severity 1, 2, 3, 4) with examples before project start; establish defect triage and remediation SLA; document acceptance criteria for each milestone before delivery; clarify what constitutes 'material conformance' and 'approved workaround'
rows.evidence: No mutually agreed Severity 1 or Severity 2 defect should remain open for the relevant milestone unless an approved workaround or deferral is documented.
rows.reason: Acceptance criteria allow Severity 1 and 2 defects to remain open with workaround or deferral; this creates quality risk especially for clinical decision support system where defects could affect patient care; 'material conformance' is subjective and may lead to acceptance disputes

rows.target: 5 seconds
rows.measurement: Rule execution and output generation for a standard patient record should target response within 5 seconds in the agreed test environment, subject to dataset size and infrastructure performance
rows.clause: Rule execution and output generation for a standard patient record should target response within 5 seconds in the agreed test environment, subject to dataset size and infrastructure performance.
rows.valueAtRisk: Physician workflow efficiency; user adoption
rows.customerDependency: Performance depends on dataset size, infrastructure performance, and test environment provided by Customer
rows.exclusions: Performance target is for test environment only; production performance depends on infrastructure and data volume
rows.recommendedAction: Define 'standard patient record' with specific data volume and complexity; establish production performance SLA separately; clarify infrastructure requirements to achieve 5-second target; test performance with realistic data volumes before production deployment
rows.evidence: Rule execution and output generation for a standard patient record should target response within 5 seconds in the agreed test environment, subject to dataset size and infrastructure performance.
rows.reason: Performance target is aspirational ('should target') not mandatory; qualified by dataset size and infrastructure performance which are outside CTIND control; no production performance SLA; 5 seconds may be too slow for real-time physician workflow

rows.target: Not stated
rows.measurement: Production availability SLAs are excluded unless separately agreed; deployment readiness artifacts will be provided under this SOW
rows.clause: Production availability SLAs are excluded unless separately agreed; deployment readiness artifacts will be provided under this SOW.
rows.valueAtRisk: Physician workflow disruption; patient care delays
rows.customerDependency: Customer must separately agree production availability SLA and support model
rows.exclusions: Production availability SLAs excluded from this SOW; production managed services beyond agreed handover excluded
rows.recommendedAction: Negotiate production availability SLA before go-live; define support model, incident response times, escalation process, and maintenance windows; clarify handover scope and ongoing support responsibilities
rows.evidence: Production availability SLAs are excluded unless separately agreed; deployment readiness artifacts will be provided under this SOW.
rows.reason: No production availability commitment creates operational risk; if system is unavailable, physicians cannot access diagnostic support; Customer may assume CTIND will provide production support but it is explicitly excluded

rows.deliverable: AWS services, AI/LLM APIs, tools, and platforms
rows.target: Customer procures at Customer cost
rows.measurement: INVKLM shall procure such third-party licenses at its cost and provide necessary access to CTIND
rows.clause: In case any third-party licenses are required for performance of Services, INVKLM shall procure such third-party licenses at its cost and provide necessary access to CTIND. Potential third-party tools/materials may include AWS services, database services, AI/LLM APIs or platforms, rules-engine libraries, identity provider, secure file transfer tools, test management tools, code repository, DevOps pipeline, monitoring tools, and clinical terminology/reference materials approved by INVKLM.
rows.valueAtRisk: Unbudgeted third-party costs; project delays if licenses not procured
rows.customerDependency: Customer must procure and provide access to all third-party licenses and tools; Customer must ensure valid licenses and authorization to provide access to CTIND
rows.exclusions: Procurement costs for AWS services, third-party AI platforms, LLM/API subscriptions, medical knowledge bases, security tools, identity platforms, monitoring tools, data labeling, or production support tools excluded from CTIND scope unless expressly included
rows.recommendedAction: Develop detailed third-party tool and license list with estimated costs before project start; establish procurement timeline and ownership; clarify which tools are required vs optional; validate Customer budget includes third-party costs; document license compliance responsibilities
rows.evidence: In case any third-party licenses are required for performance of Services, INVKLM shall procure such third-party licenses at its cost and provide necessary access to CTIND.
rows.reason: Third-party costs (AWS, AI/LLM APIs, tools) could be substantial and are excluded from USD 425,000 fixed price; Customer may not have budgeted for these costs; delays in procurement will delay project; list of potential tools is extensive and costs are undefined

rows.deliverable: Five milestones from M1 to M5
rows.measurement: Estimated due dates for each milestone; if delivery delayed due to causes attributable to INVKLM, CTIND entitled to revise delivery schedule and charge additional fees through change management
rows.clause: If delivery of any milestone is delayed due to causes attributable to INVKLM, CTIND will be entitled to revise its delivery schedule and charge additional fees to INVKLM through the change management process.
rows.valueAtRisk: Schedule delays; additional fees for Customer-caused delays
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide timely approvals, data, clinical SME input, UAT, and sign-offs per milestone schedule; Customer delays entitle CTIND to schedule revision and additional fees
rows.exclusions: CTIND not responsible for delays caused by Customer; additional fees apply for Customer-caused delays
rows.remedy: CTIND entitled to revise delivery schedule and charge additional fees through change management process
rows.recommendedAction: Define what constitutes 'causes attributable to INVKLM' with examples; establish Customer deliverable schedule with dates; clarify additional fee calculation methodology; implement weekly governance to track dependencies and risks; document Customer approval turnaround times
rows.evidence: If delivery of any milestone is delayed due to causes attributable to INVKLM, CTIND will be entitled to revise its delivery schedule and charge additional fees to INVKLM through the change management process.
rows.reason: Customer dependencies are extensive (approvals, data, clinical SME, UAT) and delays are likely; CTIND can charge additional fees but calculation method is undefined; schedule is aggressive (7 months for complex AI clinical system); no liquidated damages for CTIND delays

rows.target: Initial release covers agreed parameters and diagnostic categories documented during discovery; additional scope requires change control
rows.measurement: The initial release is a fixed-price MVP/pilot scope covering agreed parameters and diagnostic categories documented during discovery. Additional parameters, disease areas, integrations, or advanced AI capabilities require change control.
rows.clause: The initial release is a fixed-price MVP/pilot scope covering agreed parameters and diagnostic categories documented during discovery. Additional parameters, disease areas, integrations, or advanced AI capabilities require change control.
rows.valueAtRisk: Scope creep; additional costs for scope expansion
rows.customerDependency: Customer must finalize scope during discovery (M1); scope changes after discovery require change control and additional fees
rows.exclusions: Additional parameters, disease areas, integrations, advanced AI/ML model development beyond agreed scope excluded; require change control
rows.remedy: Change control process per Section 12; impact assessment and revised fees/timeline through change order
rows.recommendedAction: Finalize and document detailed scope baseline during M1 discovery with Customer sign-off; establish change control board and process; define what constitutes 'additional parameters' vs 'agreed parameters'; implement scope tracking and change log; educate Customer stakeholders on change control process
rows.evidence: The initial release is a fixed-price MVP/pilot scope covering agreed parameters and diagnostic categories documented during discovery. Additional parameters, disease areas, integrations, or advanced AI capabilities require change control.
rows.reason: Scope is defined as MVP/pilot but specific parameters and diagnostic categories are not finalized in SOW; scope will be defined during M1 discovery which creates risk of scope disagreement; Customer may expect broader scope than CTIND delivers; change control process exists but scope baseline is unclear

rows.target: Customer provides named product owner, clinical SME, security contact, infrastructure contact, AWS contact, UAT lead, and business sponsor with timely approvals
rows.measurement: Provide named product owner, clinical SME, security contact, infrastructure contact, AWS contact, UAT lead, and business sponsor. Provide timely approvals for requirements, solution design, clinical rules catalog, UI/UX, UAT, milestones, and AWS Assess/Mobilize submissions where Customer sign-off is required.
rows.clause: Provide named product owner, clinical SME, security contact, infrastructure contact, AWS contact, UAT lead, and business sponsor. Provide timely approvals for requirements, solution design, clinical rules catalog, UI/UX, UAT, milestones, and AWS Assess/Mobilize submissions where Customer sign-off is required.
rows.valueAtRisk: Project delays; additional fees for Customer-caused delays
rows.customerDependency: Customer must provide named stakeholders with authority and availability; Customer must provide timely approvals across multiple workstreams
rows.exclusions: CTIND not responsible for delays caused by Customer stakeholder unavailability or approval delays
rows.remedy: CTIND entitled to revise delivery schedule and charge additional fees through change management process if Customer causes delays
rows.recommendedAction: Obtain named stakeholder list with contact details and approval authority before project start; define approval turnaround times (e.g. 5 business days); establish escalation process for stakeholder unavailability; document stakeholder time commitment expectations; implement RACI matrix
rows.evidence: Provide named product owner, clinical SME, security contact, infrastructure contact, AWS contact, UAT lead, and business sponsor. Provide timely approvals for requirements, solution design, clinical rules catalog, UI/UX, UAT, milestones, and AWS Assess/Mobilize submissions where Customer sign-off is required.
rows.reason: Project requires seven named Customer stakeholders plus timely approvals across multiple workstreams; Customer stakeholder unavailability or slow approvals are common causes of project delays; 'timely' is not defined; no SLA for Customer approval turnaround; clinical SME availability is critical for rules validation

rows.target: CTIND retains ownership; Customer receives royalty-free license for utilization of project deliverables under this SOW
rows.measurement: CTIND shall retain ownership of all rights, title, and interests, including intellectual property rights, in such Accelerators. CTIND grants INVKLM a royalty-free license in Accelerators solely for utilization of project deliverables under this SOW.
rows.clause: CTIND shall retain ownership of all rights, title, and interests, including intellectual property rights, in such Accelerators. CTIND grants INVKLM a royalty-free license in Accelerators solely for utilization of project deliverables under this SOW.
rows.valueAtRisk: Customer ability to modify, enhance, or transfer solution; vendor lock-in
rows.customerDependency: Customer depends on CTIND-owned accelerators for solution functionality; Customer cannot modify or transfer accelerators without CTIND involvement
rows.exclusions: Customer license is limited to utilization of project deliverables under this SOW; broader use, modification, or transfer rights not granted
rows.recommendedAction: Clarify scope of 'utilization of project deliverables under this SOW' - does it include production use, modifications, enhancements, integration with other systems? Define what constitutes 'Accelerators' vs custom deliverables; negotiate broader license rights if Customer needs to modify or transfer solution; consider escrow arrangement for business continuity
rows.evidence: CTIND grants INVKLM a royalty-free license in Accelerators solely for utilization of project deliverables under this SOW.
rows.reason: Customer receives only a limited license to use CTIND accelerators for this SOW; if Customer wants to modify, enhance, port to different platform, or transfer to another vendor, CTIND consent may be required; creates vendor lock-in and limits Customer flexibility; scope of 'utilization' is unclear

rows.target: CTIND entitled to payment for all Services performed up to effective date of expiry or termination including fees for accepted milestones, work in progress, and any pre-approved investments and third-party costs
rows.measurement: CTIND shall be entitled to payment for all Services performed up to the effective date of expiry or termination including fees for accepted milestones, work in progress, and any pre-approved investments and third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of INVKLM.
rows.clause: CTIND shall be entitled to payment for all Services performed up to the effective date of expiry or termination including fees for accepted milestones, work in progress, and any pre-approved investments and third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of INVKLM. AWS funding eligibility, if any, shall not reduce amounts payable by INVKLM unless expressly agreed through written amendment.
rows.valueAtRisk: Work in progress and pre-approved investments (amount undefined)
rows.customerDependency: Customer must pay for work in progress and pre-approved investments even if project is terminated
rows.exclusions: AWS funding does not reduce Customer payment obligations on termination unless expressly agreed
rows.recommendedAction: Define how 'work in progress' will be valued (e.g. percentage of milestone fee, time and materials, effort-based); establish pre-approval process for investments and third-party costs; clarify termination notice period and wind-down obligations; negotiate cap on work in progress payment
rows.evidence: CTIND shall be entitled to payment for all Services performed up to the effective date of expiry or termination including fees for accepted milestones, work in progress, and any pre-approved investments and third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of INVKLM.
rows.reason: On termination, Customer must pay for work in progress and pre-approved investments but valuation method is undefined; could lead to payment disputes; no cap on termination payment; Customer may face unexpected costs if project is terminated mid-milestone

rows.section: Section 4.1 Detailed In-Scope Services - Explainability and audit trail
rows.deliverable: Audit trail for rule execution and user actions
rows.target: Capture input values, rule version, applicable thresholds, generated suggestion, rationale, user action, override reason, timestamp, and user identifier for traceability
rows.measurement: All diagnostic suggestions generated by the engine shall include rule/rationale traceability for physician review and audit purposes. Audit trail shall capture configured rule version, input parameter values, generated output, user action, timestamp, and user identifier for agreed workflows.
rows.clause: Capture input values, rule version, applicable thresholds, generated suggestion, rationale, user action, override reason, timestamp, and user identifier for traceability. All diagnostic suggestions generated by the engine shall include rule/rationale traceability for physician review and audit purposes.
rows.valueAtRisk: Regulatory compliance; legal defensibility; clinical governance
rows.customerDependency: Customer must define audit retention requirements, access controls, and reporting needs
rows.exclusions: Audit trail scope limited to agreed workflows; production audit reporting and analytics may require additional scope
rows.recommendedAction: Define audit retention period (e.g. 7 years for medical records); establish audit log access controls and monitoring; clarify audit reporting requirements; validate audit trail meets regulatory requirements (HIPAA, FDA 21 CFR Part 11 if applicable); test audit trail completeness and integrity
rows.evidence: Capture input values, rule version, applicable thresholds, generated suggestion, rationale, user action, override reason, timestamp, and user identifier for traceability.
rows.reason: Audit trail is critical for regulatory compliance, legal defensibility, and clinical governance; incomplete or inaccessible audit trail creates compliance risk; audit retention period not specified; audit reporting and analytics capabilities unclear; if system is used in patient care, audit trail must meet medical record retention requirements

rows.target: Authorized signatories from both parties
rows.measurement: Signature block present with fields for Authorized Signatory, Name, Title, and Date for both INVKLM and CTIND
rows.clause: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties set forth below have executed this SOW as of the Effective Date. For INVKLM: Authorized Signatory, Name, Title, Date. For CTIND: Authorized Signatory, Name, Title, Date.
rows.valueAtRisk: Contract enforceability
rows.customerDependency: Both parties must execute SOW with authorized signatories
rows.exclusions: Not applicable
rows.recommendedAction: Confirm signatory authority for both parties; obtain executed copy with signatures before project start; validate effective date aligns with project start date
rows.evidence: IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties set forth below have executed this SOW as of the Effective Date.
rows.reason: Signature block is present but actual signatures are not visible in uploaded contract; without executed signatures, SOW may not be enforceable; signatory authority should be validated

rows.target: Clinical decision support only; not intended to replace clinical judgment, function as autonomous diagnostic system, or make patient treatment decisions without physician review
rows.measurement: The solution is intended to provide clinical decision support only. It is not intended to replace clinical judgment, function as an autonomous diagnostic system, or make patient treatment decisions without review and approval by qualified physicians employed or designated by INVKLM.
rows.clause: The solution is intended to provide clinical decision support only. It is not intended to replace clinical judgment, function as an autonomous diagnostic system, or make patient treatment decisions without review and approval by qualified physicians employed or designated by INVKLM.
rows.valueAtRisk: Regulatory classification; patient safety; liability
rows.customerDependency: Customer must ensure solution is used only for intended purpose with physician review; Customer must determine regulatory classification and compliance requirements
rows.exclusions: Autonomous diagnosis, treatment prescription, medication recommendation, triage decisions, emergency alerts excluded from intended use
rows.recommendedAction: Document intended use statement in all user-facing materials and training; implement technical controls to prevent autonomous use without physician review; validate regulatory classification with FDA or other authorities; ensure physician workflow includes mandatory review step; monitor actual use to detect off-label or autonomous use
rows.evidence: The solution is intended to provide clinical decision support only. It is not intended to replace clinical judgment, function as an autonomous diagnostic system, or make patient treatment decisions without review and approval by qualified physicians employed or designated by INVKLM.
rows.reason: Intended use as clinical decision support (not autonomous diagnosis) affects regulatory classification; if solution is used autonomously or physicians rely on it without proper review, patient harm could occur; Customer must enforce intended use through workflow design and training; regulatory classification may change if actual use differs from intended use

boardSummary.statement: USD 5M ARR target appears unrealistic for 7-month project; funding failure may create payment disputes.
CTIND excludes clinical responsibility; Customer owns all patient safety and regulatory compliance obligations.
SaMD classification, FDA submission, HIPAA compliance, and security certifications explicitly excluded from scope.
10-day acceptance window with deemed acceptance creates payment risk if Customer delays UAT.
MVP scope finalized during M1 discovery; scope creep risk if parameters not clearly defined.

proceedIf.statement: Obtain written confirmation that Customer payment obligations are independent of AWS funding outcome and clarify any side agreements.
Validate Customer has clinical governance, regulatory advisory, and legal review processes to manage AI-based clinical decision support.
Confirm Customer has engaged regulatory advisors for SaMD classification, FDA submission if required, HIPAA compliance, and security certifications.
Finalize and document detailed scope baseline during M1 discovery with Customer sign-off on parameters and diagnostic categories.
Obtain named Customer stakeholders with confirmed availability and approval authority before project start.

renegotiate.statement: Extend acceptance period from 10 to 15 business days and remove deemed acceptance for clinical validation milestones.
Negotiate production availability SLA, support model, incident response times, and ongoing maintenance responsibilities.
Clarify scope of Customer license to CTIND accelerators and negotiate broader rights for modifications and enhancements.
Develop detailed third-party tool and license list with estimated costs and confirm Customer budget allocation.
Define work-in-progress valuation methodology and negotiate cap on termination payment obligations.

financialNotes.statement: USD 425,000 fixed price payable by Customer across five milestones based on acceptance or deemed acceptance.
Separate AWS Assess and Mobilize funding totaling USD 500,000 based on USD 5M ARR target; subject to AWS approval and Customer consumption.
Customer payment obligations remain regardless of AWS funding outcome unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing.
AWS services, AI/LLM APIs, tools, and licenses excluded from fixed price; Customer procures at Customer cost (amount undefined).
CTIND entitled to charge additional fees through change control if Customer causes delays or requests scope expansion.
On termination, Customer must pay for accepted milestones, work in progress, and pre-approved investments (valuation method undefined).

revenueControls.title: Milestone-based invoicing with deemed acceptance
revenueControls.detail: Each milestone invoice triggered by acceptance or deemed acceptance after 10 business days; protects revenue recognition if Customer delays UAT

revenueControls.title: Change control for scope expansion
revenueControls.detail: Additional parameters, diagnostic categories, integrations, or advanced AI capabilities require change order with revised fees

revenueControls.title: Additional fees for Customer-caused delays
revenueControls.detail: CTIND entitled to revise delivery schedule and charge additional fees if Customer causes milestone delays

revenueControls.title: Third-party cost pass-through
revenueControls.detail: Customer procures and pays for all third-party licenses, AWS services, and tools at Customer cost

revenueControls.title: Payment independence from AWS funding
revenueControls.detail: Customer invoices remain payable regardless of AWS funding outcome unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing

commercialBaseline.interpretation: Customer payment obligation across five milestones; excludes third-party costs and AWS funding
Established by AWS for Mobilize funding eligibility; appears unrealistic for 7-month project timeline
CTIND funding from AWS subject to Customer reaching USD 10K ARR (USD 400K) and 20% of ARR (USD 100K)
Undisputed invoices payable within 30 days per Agreement or as stated in SOW
Customer must accept or reject within 10 days; deemed accepted if no response; short window for clinical validation
AWS services, AI/LLM APIs, tools, licenses excluded from fixed price; Customer cost exposure undefined

leakageScenarios.scenario: AWS funding failure with payment dispute
leakageScenarios.impact: Customer may refuse payment claiming AWS funding was expected to offset cost; USD 425,000 revenue at risk
leakageScenarios.control: Contract states Customer payment obligations independent of AWS funding unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing
leakageScenarios.evidence: Unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing, AWS funding eligibility does not waive Customer payment obligations under this SOW.

leakageScenarios.impact: Customer may dispute invoice claiming deliverables not validated; milestone payment delayed or withheld
leakageScenarios.control: 10-day acceptance period with deemed acceptance clause; Customer must provide written non-conformance or milestone is accepted
leakageScenarios.evidence: If INVKLM fails to provide written acceptance or non-conformance within the Acceptance Period, the milestone shall be deemed accepted by INVKLM.

leakageScenarios.impact: Customer requests additional parameters, diagnostic categories, or integrations expecting inclusion in fixed price; margin erosion
leakageScenarios.control: SOW explicitly requires change control for scope expansion; additional scope treated as change request with revised fees
leakageScenarios.evidence: Additional parameters, disease areas, integrations, or advanced AI capabilities require change control.

leakageScenarios.scenario: Customer delays without additional fee recovery
leakageScenarios.impact: Customer delays providing data, approvals, or stakeholders; project timeline extends but CTIND cannot recover additional costs
leakageScenarios.control: Contract entitles CTIND to revise delivery schedule and charge additional fees through change management if Customer causes delays
leakageScenarios.evidence: If delivery of any milestone is delayed due to causes attributable to INVKLM, CTIND will be entitled to revise its delivery schedule and charge additional fees to INVKLM through the change management process.

leakageScenarios.impact: AWS services, AI/LLM APIs, or tool costs exceed Customer budget; Customer refuses to procure or delays procurement
leakageScenarios.control: Contract requires Customer to procure third-party licenses at Customer cost; CTIND not responsible for procurement or cost
leakageScenarios.evidence: In case any third-party licenses are required for performance of Services, INVKLM shall procure such third-party licenses at its cost and provide necessary access to CTIND.

penaltyExposure.High: No service credits or penalties stated in uploaded contract for CTIND performance failures. Customer payment obligations and CTIND additional fee entitlements are the primary commercial mechanisms.
penaltyExposure.Medium: Customer-caused delays entitle CTIND to additional fees through change control but calculation methodology is undefined, creating potential dispute risk.
penaltyExposure.Low: Deemed acceptance after 10 business days protects CTIND revenue but may create Customer relationship risk if deliverables have quality issues.

recos.contract: Section 13.4 AWS Funding Dependencies and Clarifications
recos.action: Remove ambiguity about Customer payment obligations and AWS funding interaction
recos.original: Unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing, AWS funding eligibility does not waive Customer payment obligations under this SOW. Customer invoices remain payable as per the Agreement and this SOW.
recos.rewrite: Customer payment obligations under this SOW are absolute and unconditional. Customer shall pay all milestone invoices as per the payment terms regardless of AWS funding eligibility, AWS approval outcome, or AWS funding receipt by CTIND. No side agreement, oral understanding, or AWS funding arrangement shall reduce or waive Customer payment obligations unless documented in a written amendment to this SOW signed by authorized representatives of both parties.

recos.action: Extend acceptance period and remove deemed acceptance for clinical validation milestones
recos.original: Within ten (10) business days from delivery of each milestone, INVKLM shall complete user acceptance testing for the relevant milestone as per the acceptance criteria mutually agreed between the parties and provide written acceptance or non-conformance for the same. If INVKLM fails to provide written acceptance or non-conformance within the Acceptance Period, the milestone shall be deemed accepted by INVKLM.
recos.rewrite: Within fifteen (15) business days from delivery of each milestone, INVKLM shall complete user acceptance testing for the relevant milestone as per the acceptance criteria mutually agreed between the parties and provide written acceptance or non-conformance for the same. For Milestone 1 (Discovery and Clinical Rules Catalog) and Milestone 5 (UAT and Deployment Readiness), which require clinical SME validation, the acceptance period shall be twenty (20) business days. If INVKLM fails to provide written acceptance or non-conformance within the Acceptance Period, CTIND may send a written reminder. If INVKLM fails to respond within five (5) business days of the reminder, the milestone shall be deemed accepted by INVKLM.

recos.action: Add realistic ARR target and Customer cooperation obligations
recos.original: The ARR for this project is established by AWS at USD 5,000,000 for the purpose of the Mobilize funding reference under this SOW. CTIND will procure funding from AWS based on work performed and AWS eligibility, per the following milestone schedule applicable to both SOWs: Milestone 1 Customer reaches USD 10,000 ARR. CTIND eligible to request first payment from AWS = USD 400,000. Milestone 2 Customer reaches 20% of ARR. CTIND eligible to request remaining funding from AWS = USD 100,000.
recos.rewrite: The ARR for this project is established by AWS at USD 5,000,000 for the purpose of the Mobilize funding reference under this SOW. The parties acknowledge that achieving this ARR target within the 7-month project timeline is aspirational and depends on Customer AWS consumption, production deployment timing, and AWS approval. INVKLM shall cooperate in good faith with CTIND and AWS by: (a) providing AWS account ID details and consumption reports within 5 business days of request; (b) executing required AWS program documentation within 10 business days of request; (c) providing written sign-offs for AWS funding submissions within 5 business days of request; and (d) promptly escalating any AWS approval or process issues. If AWS funding is not approved or ARR targets are not achieved, Customer payment obligations under Section 13.1 remain unchanged. CTIND will procure funding from AWS based on work performed and AWS eligibility, per the following milestone schedule: Milestone 1 Customer reaches USD 10,000 ARR. CTIND eligible to request first payment from AWS = USD 400,000. Milestone 2 Customer reaches 20% of ARR (USD 1,000,000). CTIND eligible to request remaining funding from AWS = USD 100,000.

recos.action: Add explicit Customer acknowledgment and indemnification for clinical use
recos.original: CTIND shall not assume clinical responsibility for diagnoses, treatments, prescriptions, patient outcomes, or physician decisions made using outputs generated by the system. INVKLM shall be responsible for medical review, validation, clinical governance, regulatory assessment, production release approval, and intended-use determination.
recos.rewrite: CTIND shall not assume clinical responsibility for diagnoses, treatments, prescriptions, patient outcomes, or physician decisions made using outputs generated by the system. INVKLM acknowledges and agrees that: (a) INVKLM is solely responsible for medical review, validation, clinical governance, regulatory assessment, production release approval, and intended-use determination; (b) INVKLM shall ensure all diagnostic suggestions are reviewed and approved by qualified licensed physicians before use in patient care; (c) INVKLM shall implement technical and procedural controls to prevent autonomous use of the system without physician review; (d) INVKLM shall determine and comply with all applicable regulatory requirements including FDA medical device classification, HIPAA, and state medical practice laws; and (e) INVKLM shall indemnify and hold harmless CTIND from any claims, damages, or liabilities arising from patient care decisions, clinical use of the system, regulatory non-compliance, or failure to implement required physician review workflows.

recos.action: Add production availability SLA and support model
recos.original: Production availability SLAs are excluded unless separately agreed; deployment readiness artifacts will be provided under this SOW.
recos.rewrite: Production availability SLA: CTIND shall provide production support for twelve (12) months following production deployment at no additional cost, subject to the following SLA: (a) System Availability: 99.5% uptime measured monthly, excluding scheduled maintenance windows (maximum 4 hours per month with 48 hours advance notice); (b) Incident Response: Severity 1 (system down) - 2 hour response, 8 hour resolution target; Severity 2 (major function impaired) - 4 hour response, 24 hour resolution target; Severity 3 (minor issue) - 1 business day response, 5 business days resolution target; (c) Service Credit: If monthly availability falls below 99.5%, Customer receives service credit of 5% of monthly support fee (pro-rated) for each 0.5% below target, up to maximum 25% monthly credit. After twelve months, ongoing production support shall be subject to separate support agreement.

recos.action: Clarify scope of Customer license and add modification rights
recos.original: CTIND shall retain ownership of all rights, title, and interests, including intellectual property rights, in such Accelerators. CTIND grants INVKLM a royalty-free license in Accelerators solely for utilization of project deliverables under this SOW.
recos.rewrite: CTIND shall retain ownership of all rights, title, and interests, including intellectual property rights, in such Accelerators. CTIND grants INVKLM a perpetual, irrevocable, worldwide, royalty-free license to: (a) use, execute, and display the Accelerators as incorporated in the project deliverables for INVKLM's internal business purposes; (b) modify, enhance, and create derivative works of the Accelerators as incorporated in the project deliverables; (c) integrate the Accelerators with INVKLM's other systems and applications; and (d) engage third-party service providers to maintain, support, and enhance the project deliverables including the incorporated Accelerators. INVKLM shall not: (i) distribute, sublicense, or transfer the Accelerators as standalone components separate from the project deliverables; or (ii) use the Accelerators to develop competing products or services for third parties.

recos.action: Define work-in-progress valuation and cap termination payment
recos.original: CTIND shall be entitled to payment for all Services performed up to the effective date of expiry or termination including fees for accepted milestones, work in progress, and any pre-approved investments and third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of INVKLM.
recos.rewrite: CTIND shall be entitled to payment for all Services performed up to the effective date of expiry or termination including: (a) fees for accepted milestones; (b) work in progress for the current milestone calculated as the milestone fee multiplied by the percentage of milestone deliverables completed as of the termination date, up to a maximum of 80% of the milestone fee; and (c) pre-approved investments and third-party costs incurred for or on behalf of INVKLM, supported by invoices or receipts. Total termination payment shall not exceed the sum of accepted milestone fees plus 80% of the current milestone fee plus documented pre-approved costs. CTIND shall provide detailed breakdown of work in progress and costs within ten (10) business days of termination.

recos.action: Add third-party cost estimate and budget confirmation
recos.original: In case any third-party licenses are required for performance of Services, INVKLM shall procure such third-party licenses at its cost and provide necessary access to CTIND. Potential third-party tools/materials may include AWS services, database services, AI/LLM APIs or platforms, rules-engine libraries, identity provider, secure file transfer tools, test management tools, code repository, DevOps pipeline, monitoring tools, and clinical terminology/reference materials approved by INVKLM.
recos.rewrite: INVKLM shall procure the following third-party licenses and services at INVKLM cost and provide necessary access to CTIND: (a) AWS services (estimated USD 50,000 - 100,000 for development, testing, and initial production); (b) AI/LLM API services if required (estimated USD 10,000 - 30,000); (c) identity provider and security tools (estimated USD 5,000 - 15,000); (d) monitoring and DevOps tools (estimated USD 5,000 - 10,000); and (e) clinical terminology/reference materials if required (cost TBD based on selection). CTIND shall provide detailed third-party tool and license requirements with cost estimates during Milestone 1 (Discovery). INVKLM shall confirm budget availability and procurement approach within ten (10) business days of receiving the requirements. If INVKLM does not procure required third-party tools within agreed timelines, CTIND shall be entitled to revise the delivery schedule without penalty.

recos.action: Add detailed scope baseline documentation requirement
recos.original: The initial release is a fixed-price MVP/pilot scope covering agreed parameters and diagnostic categories documented during discovery. Additional parameters, disease areas, integrations, or advanced AI capabilities require change control.
recos.rewrite: The initial release is a fixed-price MVP/pilot scope covering parameters and diagnostic categories documented and approved during Milestone 1 (Discovery). The Discovery deliverable shall include a detailed Scope Baseline Document specifying: (a) complete list of blood test parameters (maximum 50 parameters); (b) diagnostic categories and suggestion labels (maximum 20 categories); (c) reference ranges and rule thresholds for each parameter; (d) data sources and integration points (maximum 3 sources); (e) user roles and access levels (maximum 5 roles); and (f) functional capabilities and acceptance criteria for each milestone. INVKLM shall review and provide written approval of the Scope Baseline Document within ten (10) business days of delivery. Any requests for additional parameters, diagnostic categories, data sources, user roles, integrations, or advanced AI capabilities beyond the approved Scope Baseline shall require change control with impact assessment and revised fees.

recos.action: Add Customer approval turnaround SLA and escalation process
recos.original: Provide timely approvals for requirements, solution design, clinical rules catalog, UI/UX, UAT, milestones, and AWS Assess/Mobilize submissions where Customer sign-off is required.
recos.rewrite: INVKLM shall provide timely approvals for requirements, solution design, clinical rules catalog, UI/UX, UAT, milestones, and AWS Assess/Mobilize submissions where Customer sign-off is required, according to the following turnaround times: (a) Requirements and design documents: ten (10) business days; (b) Clinical rules catalog and medical content: fifteen (15) business days with clinical SME review; (c) UI/UX prototypes: five (5) business days; (d) Milestone deliverables: per Section 10 Acceptance Process; (e) AWS program documentation: five (5) business days; and (f) Change requests: ten (10) business days. If INVKLM does not provide approval or written feedback within the specified turnaround time, CTIND may escalate to the INVKLM Business Sponsor. If no response is received within five (5) business days of escalation, the item shall be deemed approved and CTIND may proceed. INVKLM approval delays exceeding the turnaround times by more than ten (10) business days shall entitle CTIND to revise the delivery schedule and charge additional fees through change control.

assumptions.assumption: All values extracted directly from uploaded SOW dated 01-Jun-2026. No external agreements or side letters analyzed. Master Services Agreement dated 01-Jun-2026 referenced but not uploaded; terms assumed to be standard commercial terms unless SOW specifies otherwise.

Classified as EM4 – Fixed Price based on explicit 'Fixed Price with milestone-based invoicing' label, total fixed price of USD 425,000, CTIND ownership of delivery scope and milestones, acceptance-based payment triggers, and change control requirement for scope expansion. Pricing model 'Fixed Price' is consistent with EM4 classification.

Contract value is USD 425,000 (Customer payment obligation). AWS Assess and Mobilize funding totaling USD 500,000 is separate CTIND revenue from AWS, not Customer payment. Total project value could be viewed as USD 925,000 (Customer USD 425K + AWS USD 500K) but Customer value at risk is USD 425,000. Third-party costs (AWS services, AI/LLM APIs, tools) are excluded from fixed price and borne by Customer at undefined additional cost.

Contract Value at Risk allocated by row count: High risks (8 rows) = 40% = USD 170,000; Medium risks (11 rows) = 55% = USD 233,750; Low risks (1 row) = 5% = USD 21,250. Adjusted to High 50% / Medium 40% / Low 10% per instructions: High USD 212,500, Medium USD 170,000, Low USD 42,500. This is directional allocation for visualization; actual financial exposure depends on specific risk materialization.

USD 5,000,000 ARR target established by AWS appears extremely high relative to USD 425,000 SOW value and 7-month timeline. Achieving 20% of ARR (USD 1,000,000 annual consumption) within project period is unlikely unless Customer has substantial existing AWS consumption or rapid production scale-up. AWS funding eligibility depends on Customer cooperation, AWS approval, and consumption validation outside CTIND control. Contract states Customer payment obligations are independent of AWS funding 'unless expressly agreed otherwise in writing' - this phrase creates ambiguity about potential side agreements.

No service credits, penalties, or liquidated damages stated in uploaded contract for CTIND performance failures (availability, response time, milestone delays). Performance target of 5 seconds is aspirational ('should target') not mandatory. Production availability SLAs explicitly excluded. Primary commercial mechanisms are Customer payment obligations (milestone-based) and CTIND entitlement to additional fees for Customer-caused delays.

Page numbers extracted from [[PAGE n]] markers in uploaded PDF. Evidence quotes are verbatim from contract text to enable page location. For DOCX uploads without markers, pageNo would be 'Not available'.

Solution is AI-based diagnostic support tool for physician use in patient care. Regulatory classification (Software as a Medical Device under FDA, EU MDR) depends on intended use and risk classification determined by Customer. HIPAA compliance mandatory for PHI handling. Clinical validation, regulatory submission, medical device registration, and patient safety responsibility explicitly allocated to Customer. CTIND liability exclusion for clinical outcomes may not be fully enforceable if system defects contribute to patient harm.

Detailed scope (specific blood test parameters, diagnostic categories, reference ranges, rule count) will be finalized during Milestone 1 Discovery. SOW provides example parameter categories in Annexure 2 but states 'Included only for parameters approved during discovery.' This creates scope definition risk and potential for scope disagreement after project start.

Project success depends heavily on Customer providing: clinical content (parameters, rules, ranges), sample data, clinical SME approvals, timely sign-offs, AWS cooperation, stakeholder availability, and third-party tool procurement. No specific SLAs or turnaround times for Customer deliverables. Customer delays entitle CTIND to additional fees but calculation methodology undefined.

Confidence scores reflect: (a) clarity and specificity of contract language; (b) presence of supporting evidence in uploaded contract; (c) degree of ambiguity or dependency on external factors; (d) completeness of commercial/legal/technical terms. Scores range 80-95 reflecting strong contract documentation with some areas of ambiguity (AWS funding interaction, scope baseline, third-party costs, termination payment valuation).

Signature block present but actual signatures not visible in uploaded contract. Contract effective date is 01-Jun-2026 which is in the past relative to current date 15-Jul-2026, indicating contract should be executed. Execution evidence should be validated before relying on contract enforceability.

Contract involves healthcare data (blood test parameters, patient records) which may include Protected Health Information (PHI) under HIPAA. Contract requires Customer to provide anonymized, pseudonymized, or authorized data for development/testing. Production PHI access subject to Customer-approved controls. Business Associate Agreement (BAA) may be required if CTIND will access PHI. Data handling protocol and HIPAA compliance controls should be established before project start.
